channel.title: Abhishek Murali — Data Musings
channel.description: I build the data platform behind the decisions — BigQuery, Airflow, Looker — from zero, end to end, with contracts and governance baked in.

item.title: Beyond A/B Tests: Using Cohort Analysis to Model Product Commitment Before You Build It
item.description: When the cost of a wrong product bet is high, cohort analysis of past behaviour is more useful than a speculative experiment. Here's how retention curves moved a K4 number from 26% to 48% — before a single engineer was tasked.

item.title: The SFF Ratio: Telling Abuse from Operational Failure with Segmentation
item.description: A spike in Self-Force-Finish events looked like abuse. Segmentation revealed it was high-value customers signalling a GPS black hole — and the fix belonged in product, not policy.

item.title: Looker vs. Power BI: Aligning BI Tools to How Fast Stakeholders Need to Decide
item.description: A single BI tool serving all stakeholders is a governance failure in waiting. The right frame is decision latency — how fast each audience actually needs to act on what they see.

item.title: From Zero to KPI: Architecting a BigQuery Warehouse for IoT Telemetry
item.description: The architectural decisions behind standing up a production-grade GCP warehouse that handles high-velocity IoT streams and still answers an exec's question in seconds.

item.title: Data Contracts as GDPR: How Data Quality Hardens PII Compliance
item.description: Reliability engineering and privacy compliance are usually treated as separate workstreams. They're the same discipline wearing two hats.

item.title: Swaps/Bike/Week: Normalising Demand to Find Fleets Under Real Stress
item.description: Raw volume tells you where things are busy. A normalised metric tells you where they're about to break — and where to put your next pound of capital.
